items.briefing:
Australian organisations are pulling back on AI agents due to governance concerns, with customers also losing confidence in AI used by banks, insurers, and other regulated industries — a trend worth noting if your organisation uses AI-facing services. Separately, sports betting apps are being flagged as active fraud targets, so staff using these apps on personal devices should be cautious, especially if those devices also access work systems.

A fileless malware strain called Phantom Stealer is actively targeting saved browser credentials, meaning passwords stored in Chrome or Edge could be at risk. **Practical tip:** Avoid saving work passwords in your browser — use your organisation's approved password manager instead.

featured.title: Codenotary flags 210,000 risky AI agent actions daily
featured.quote: Enterprise AI systems pose major security risks, with 7% of monitored actions raising concerns daily.
